sentence: El ferreiru fizo un cabeiru nuevu.
ipa: el feˈrei̯ɾu ˈfiθu un kaˈβei̯ɾu ˈnwebu
gloss_en: The blacksmith made a new roof-beam.
notes: Western decreasing diphthong ⟨ei⟩ [ei̯] 'ferreiru','cabeiru' — the most diagnostic western Leonese marker, absent in eastern varieties — and final -u 'nuevu' per morala_egido2009; ⟨z⟩ [θ] 'fizo' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: Comimos queisu con pan de outru día.
ipa: koˈmimos ˈkei̯su kon ˈpan de ˈou̯tɾu ˈdia
gloss_en: We ate cheese with bread from another day.
notes: Western ⟨ei⟩ 'queisu' and ⟨ou⟩ [ou̯] 'outru' decreasing diphthongs plus final -u per morala_egido2009; -o>-u closure per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: La cousa nun ta cocida entováa.
ipa: la ˈkou̯sa ˈnun ˈta koˈθiða entoˈbaa
gloss_en: The thing is not yet cooked.
notes: Western ⟨ou⟩ [ou̯] 'cousa' per morala_egido2009; ⟨c⟩ before front vowel [θ] 'cocida' and negator 'nun' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: Chovía muitu na Cabreira aquel outuenu.
ipa: tʃoˈbia ˈmwitu na kaˈbɾei̯ɾa aˈkel ou̯ˈtwenu
gloss_en: It rained a lot in Cabreira that autumn.
notes: Western ⟨ei⟩ 'Cabreira', ⟨ou⟩ 'outuenu' and -CT->/it/ 'muitu' per morala_egido2009; ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'Chovía' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: El rapaz cueye la fueya del carbayeiru.
ipa: el raˈpaθ kweˈʝe la fweˈʝa del kaɾbaˈʝei̯ɾu
gloss_en: The boy picks the leaf of the oak.
notes: Tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'cueye','fueya', -LJ->[ʝ] and western ⟨ei⟩ 'carbayeiru' per morala_egido2009; ⟨z⟩ [θ] 'rapaz' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: La muyer merca fariña na feira.
ipa: la muˈʝeɾ ˈmeɾka faˈɾiɲa na ˈfei̯ɾa
gloss_en: The woman buys flour at the fair.
notes: ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'fariña' and western ⟨ei⟩ 'feira' per morala_egido2009; -LJ->[ʝ] 'muyer' and 'mercar' (< to buy) per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: ¿Cuántos años tien to pai?
ipa: ˈkuantos ˈaɲos ˈtjen ˈtu ˈpaj
gloss_en: How many years does your father have?
notes: ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'años' and diphthong 'tien' per morala_egido2009; 'pai' (< padre) per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: El gatu ñeiru duerme cabo del llar.
ipa: el ˈɡatu ˈɲei̯ɾu ˈdweɾme ˈkaβu del ˈʎaɾ
gloss_en: The cat sleeps by the hearth.
notes: Word-initial N->[ɲ] 'ñeiru' with western ⟨ei⟩, L->[ʎ] 'llar' per morala_egido2009; tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'duerme' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: ¡Baxa pola caleya y pieslla la portiella!
ipa: ˈbaʃa ˈpola kaˈleʝa ʝ ˈpjesʎa la poˈɾtjeʎa
gloss_en: Come down the lane and shut the little gate!
notes: -LJ->[ʝ] 'caleya' and -LL->[ʎ] 'pieslla','portiella' per morala_egido2009; ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'Baxa' and tonic Ĕ>/ie/ 'portiella' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: Trés vacas pacen na braña.
ipa: ˈtɾɛs ˈbakas paˈθen na ˈbɾaɲa
gloss_en: Three cows graze on the high pasture.
notes: Western plural -as 'vacas' (vs central -es) and ⟨ñ⟩ [ɲ] 'braña' per morala_egido2009; ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'pacen' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: La ñeve cubriú tol prau de Laciana.
ipa: la ˈɲebe kuˈbɾiu ˈtol ˈpɾaw de laˈθjana
gloss_en: The snow covered the whole meadow of Laciana.
notes: Word-initial N->[ɲ] 'ñeve' per morala_egido2009; falling diphthong 'prau' and ⟨z⟩... betacism 'ñeve' [b] per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: El molineiru moliú el centenu.
ipa: el moliˈnei̯ɾu moˈliu el θeˈntenu
gloss_en: The miller ground the rye.
notes: Western ⟨ei⟩ 'molineiru' and final -u 'centenu' per morala_egido2009; ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'centenu' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: Chegóu tarde a casa pola nueite.
ipa: tʃeˈɣɔu ˈtaɾde a ˈkasa ˈpola ˈnweite
gloss_en: He arrived home late in the night.
notes: Western ⟨ou⟩ 'Chegóu', -CT->/it/ 'nueite' and tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'nueite' per morala_egido2009; ⟨ch⟩ [tʃ] 'Chegóu' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: La fonte cuerre cerca del teixu vieyu.
ipa: la ˈfonte ˈkwere ˈθeɾka del ˈtei̯ʃu bjeˈʝu
gloss_en: The spring runs near the old yew.
notes: Tonic Ŏ>/ue/ 'cuerre', western ⟨ei⟩ 'teixu' and -LJ->[ʝ] 'vieyu' per morala_egido2009; ⟨c⟩ [θ] 'cerca' and trill [r] per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: El perru lladra na corrada tola nueite.
ipa: el ˈperu ˈʎadɾa na koˈraða ˈtola ˈnweite
gloss_en: The dog barks in the yard all night.
notes: Word-initial L->[ʎ] 'lladra' and -CT->/it/ 'nueite' per morala_egido2009; trill [r] 'perru','corrada' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: Falóu con Xuan na romeiría.
ipa: faˈlɔu kon ˈʃwan na romei̯ˈɾia
gloss_en: He talked with Xuan at the pilgrimage.
notes: Western ⟨ou⟩ 'Falóu', F- retention and ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'Xuan' per morala_egido2009; western ⟨ei⟩ 'romeiría' and trill [r] per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: El caldeiru ta chenu d'augua fría.
ipa: el kaˈldei̯ɾu ˈta ˈtʃenu ˈd ˈawga ˈfɾia
gloss_en: The bucket is full of cold water.
notes: Western ⟨ei⟩ 'caldeiru', PL->[tʃ] 'chenu' (< plenu) and final -u per morala_egido2009; 'augua' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: La primeira casa ye la del ferreiru.
ipa: la pɾiˈmei̯ɾa ˈkasa ˈʝe la del feˈrei̯ɾu
gloss_en: The first house is the blacksmith's.
notes: Western ⟨ei⟩ 'primeira','ferreiru', copula 'ye' and final -u per morala_egido2009; trill [r] 'ferreiru' per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: Nun quiero más pan nin queisu.
ipa: ˈnun ˈkjeɾu ˈmas ˈpan nin ˈkei̯su
gloss_en: I don't want more bread or cheese.
notes: Negator 'nun'/'nin' and diphthong 'quiero' per morala_egido2009; western ⟨ei⟩ 'queisu' and final -u per garcia_arias2003.

sentence: Diez ovellas baxan pol monte.
ipa: ˈdjeθ oˈbeʎas baˈʃan ˈpol ˈmonte
gloss_en: Ten sheep go down the hillside.
notes: -LL->[ʎ] 'ovellas' and diphthong 'Diez' [dje-] per morala_egido2009; ⟨x⟩ [ʃ] 'baxan' and ⟨z⟩ [θ] 'Diez' per garcia_arias2003.
